steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The statement says "the degree of this difference varied across the stories" and asks which pair of stories best illustrates that variation. The correct pair must show two stories where the gap between spoiled and unspoiled enjoyment ratings is noticeably different from each other—one story with a large gap and one with a small (or smaller) gap.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Blitzed" and "Plumbing.": if both stories show similar-sized gaps between spoiled and unspoiled ratings, this pair does not illustrate variation in the degree of the spoiler effect. ✗
  • Choice B — "Owl Creek Bridge" and "A Chess Problem.": these two stories show the most contrasting gap sizes in the graph—one with a notably small difference and one with a notably large difference between spoiled and unspoiled ratings—directly illustrating how the spoiler effect varies. ✓
  • Choice C — "Blitzed" and "A Chess Problem.": if both stories show large spoiler-effect gaps, this pair does not contrast variation; it would illustrate similarity instead. ✗
  • Choice D — "The Calm" and "Plumbing.": if both stories show similarly sized gaps, this pair also fails to demonstrate meaningful variation in the spoiler effect. ✗
That is the correct answer.
